title: What factors could influence FII buying behaviour in Indian equities?
summary: An FII who invested in Indian equities at the start of 2024 and exited in early 2025 with a modest Sensex gain would have earned barely 0.61% in dollar terms after rupee depreciation — against US 10-year bond yields that were above 4.5% through much of 2024, and remain at 4.08% as of February 2026.

summary: From co-investment vehicles and AI-only schemes to ₹1,000 social funds, Sebi’s AIF reforms expand flexibility for wealthy investors while raising fresh questions on liquidity and risk.

title: Gainers & Losers: Indigo, Paras Defence among 7 big movers on Wednesday
summary: Indian markets tumbled as the Iran-Israel war escalated, dragging energy stocks lower. Nifty and Sensex fell sharply, while select stocks like Coal India and Bajaj Hindusthan Sugar bucked the trend.

title: Indian bond yield trades near 3-week high on crude spike
summary: Indian government bond yields climbed to nearly a three-week high in afternoon trade on Wednesday, tracking a sharp spike in global crude oil prices amid escalating tensions in the Middle East.

title: L&T shares crash 7% as escalating Israel-Iran war spooks investors
summary: Larsen & Toubro shares plunged over 7% amid escalating Middle East conflict, raising investor concerns about project disruptions. The region accounts for a significant portion of L&T's order book. With tanker traffic halted through the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial oil route, further impact on

title: Oil surge drags Indian rupee to record low; importers scramble to hedge
summary: The Indian rupee fell to ​its all-time low on Wednesday, as ​mounting concerns of a prolonged conflict along a major oil-and-gas ​corridor in the Middle East heightened the risk of a deeper energy shock and unsettled markets.

title: Europe's STOXX 600 ticks up, Spain hit by US trade jitters
summary: Spanish stocks slid on Wednesday after fresh trade-embargo threats from ​the White House, while other regional benchmarks inched higher, after a bruising global sell-off that dragged stocks to more-than-one-month lows amid fears the Middle East conflict could widen.

title: Balrampur Chini, other sugar stocks rally up to 18% as oil prices soar. Here’s why
summary: Indian sugar stocks rallied up to 18% after Strait of Hormuz disruptions lifted crude prices, raising expectations that Brazil will divert more cane to ethanol over sugar. Tighter global supply outlook and firmer sugar prices fueled sharp gains across sector counters.

title: 11 midcap stocks plunge up to 50% from 52-week highs; how FIIs & MFs are positioned
summary: Indian equities slide over 6% from peaks as US–Israel–Iran tensions push crude to 19-month highs. Midcaps are hit hardest, with several stocks plunging 40–50%. FII and mutual fund activity shows selective buying even as broader risk appetite weakens.

title: Tata Steel, Vedanta, Hindustan Copper fall up to 8% as Iran-Israel war hits metals' prospects
summary: Tata Steel plunged 8% to Rs 194.43 amid a broader commodities selloff and sharp decline in metal stocks. Escalating Iran–Israel tensions, soaring crude prices, FII selling and slowdown fears dragged Sensex and Nifty lower despite the stock’s strong one-year outperformance.

title: Buy the fear? Indian markets delivered strong returns after past wars even as Middle East tensions roil stocks
summary: Indian equity benchmarks plunged as West Asia tensions and a Strait of Hormuz shutdown threat jolted global markets and spiked crude prices. Despite near-term risks to energy supplies and India’s import exposure, historical data show strong two-year rebounds after past geopolitical conflicts.

title: Sensex will touch 1,07,000 by December, cheapest in gold terms: Morgan Stanley
summary: Indian stocks are experiencing a disconnect between market performance and economic fundamentals. Global firm Morgan Stanley sees this as a buying opportunity. They project the Sensex could reach 107,000 by December 2026 in a bull-case scenario. This outlook is supported by improving macroeconomics
